patches.summary: Add combinable --out-of-range and --section filters to `export pdf-for-doctor`; filtered set drives the PDF subtitle, summary counts, and JSON envelope.
patches.reason: Doctors want a focused report (only out-of-range, or one section like Heart/Liver), not the full 128-biomarker dump.
patches.validated_outcome: Live: ALL=128, --out-of-range=23, --section Nutrients=26, combined=4, unknown section returns a friendly notFoundErr.

patches.summary: Sync the headline /results-report endpoint (and store /recommendations) as single envelopes under a fixed key; results-report replaces the ID-less /results list in the default sync set.
patches.reason: loadAllResults reads resource_type='results-report' but sync only fetched /results (ID-less, stored 0 rows), so every transcend command (PDF, trends, goat, oscillating, recs) had no data. This is retro item #3, which was believed worked-around but was live and unfixed.
patches.validated_outcome: Live full sync stores results-report=1 and recommendations=1; PDF/trends/recs now return real data.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Single-envelope (non-paginated, ID-less) resources should be sync-able without a per-CLI special-case
deferred_to_upstream.reason: The generic pagination/ID-extraction path drops single-object endpoints like results-report and recommendations; the generator should recognize the spec's report endpoint as the sync target.

patches.summary: Rewrite `recommendations stale` for the real nested /recommendations shape (category groups -> items with name + Quest-code Biomarkers); join targets to results by Quest code; add --group and --limit; repurpose --min-rounds as out-of-optimal persistence (the endpoint has no issued-date).
patches.reason: The generated command assumed flat recs with id/title/body/biomarkerId/createdAt — none of which exist. The live payload is dietary/supplement guidance tagged with Quest codes and no timestamp, so age-based staleness is impossible; outcome-based (target still out-of-optimal) is the honest signal. This is retro 'recommendations stale field-mismatch'.
patches.validated_outcome: Live: joins all 10 distinct rec Quest codes to biomarkers; --group supplements and --min-rounds N filter correctly; QuestCode field added to resultRow for the join.

patches.summary: Fix `biomarkers oscillating` to exclude draws without a defined Function-optimal range from the crossing count (new hasOptimal helper + extracted optimalCrossings), so undefined-optimal draws no longer masquerade as in-range and absorb real crossings.
patches.reason: optimalSign returns 0 for BOTH 'in range' and 'no optimal range defined', so an undefined-optimal draw between an above and a below reading silently swallowed the crossing — the 'oscillating inconclusive' gap. optimalSign is left unchanged (other call sites depend on its semantics).
patches.validated_outcome: Unit test asserts an undefined-optimal draw between above/below still counts the crossing; full suite green.

patches.summary: Correct README + SKILL auth docs: email/password `auth login` is blocked at the Function Health project level and `auth login --chrome` is unreliable; document the DevTools token extraction -> `auth set-token` path and add a Known Gaps section.
patches.reason: The generated docs claimed email/password login works and auto-refreshes; in reality Firebase REST password sign-in is blocked (retro #1) and the chrome flow doesn't capture a refresh token (retro #2). Also notes that a pure-Go IndexedDB LevelDB reader is the planned long-term auth fix.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Pure-Go IndexedDB LevelDB reader to extract Firebase tokens from Chrome without a browser shell-out
deferred_to_upstream.reason: browser-use against a sandboxed profile is unreliable; reading Chrome's IndexedDB directly would make `auth login --chrome` durable.

patches.summary: Cache /notes as a single envelope (it's ID-less, like results-report) and rewrite `bundle`'s notes + recommendations parsing for the real shapes: notes = {notes:[{note,category}]} matched by the biomarker's category or a whole-word, distinctive-name prose mention with HTML-entity decode; recommendations = nested envelope joined by Quest code.
patches.reason: bundle parsed notes as {body,text,biomarker} and recs as {title,body,biomarker} — neither matches reality, so both sections were empty. notes also never cached (no extractable ID). Whole-word + distinctive-name gating prevents a short biomarker name like 'Lead'/'Iron' from matching the English verb in note prose.
patches.validated_outcome: Live: bundle Hematocrit returns 3 relevant Blood notes (entities decoded) + omega-3 food recs; bundle Lead falls back to category-only (no 'lead to'/'lead a' verb false-positives).

patches.summary: Add Store.ReplaceSingleton and use it for the single-envelope resources so each holds exactly one canonical row.
patches.reason: An earlier sync stored recommendations/results-report under id=<resource-name> via the generic id-extraction fallback; the new code stored id='current', leaving TWO rows per type. loadAllResults concatenates every results-report row, so draws were silently DOUBLE-COUNTED across every transcend command (bundle/trend/goat/oscillating/pdf). ReplaceSingleton atomically purges prior rows + FTS entries before writing the canonical row.
patches.validated_outcome: Live: exactly 1 row per type after --full; ApoB trend shows 2 draws (was 4); PDF 128 biomarkers / 3 rounds.

patches.summary: Propagate the existing rich mcp-descriptions.json overrides for notifications_list and schedules_list into the generated MCP surface via mcp-sync.
patches.reason: tools-audit flagged two thin-mcp-description findings; the rich overrides already existed in mcp-descriptions.json but had not been applied to tools-manifest.json / internal/mcp/tools.go.
